setting.location: Southwest Japan seas, between Kyushu and Nansei Islands
setting.climate: warm and humid, four seasons, mild extremes
setting.visual_baseline: Animal Crossing soft pastels — rounded forms, low-saturation warm tones; pre-restoration grey-white, each repair raises saturation one step
setting.audio_direction: ocean waves, wooden geta footsteps, wind chimes, shamisen, cicada changes; dialogue uses Japanese mumble SFX, no full voice

protagonist.backstory: Five years of Tokyo advertising burnout; received a mysterious ink-brushed letter from lighthouse keeper Maruyama Wataru; took long leave and arrived by ferry
protagonist.narrative_vehicle: In-game diary — short entries, precise copywriter's language, always circling around the self

romantic_interest.identity: Granddaughter of lighthouse keeper Maruyama Wataru and Maruyama Fuyuko
romantic_interest.backstory: Left island 3 years ago to study design in Osaka; returned by ferry the same day Ritsu arrived
romantic_interest.appearance_notes: Short black hair, wooden hairpin (grandfather's), light linen shirt, worn straw sandals, fisherman's ring on a cord at her chest
romantic_interest.personality: Outwardly bright, inwardly deep; knows the island like her own palm; light laugh, heavy gaze; communicates through action not words
romantic_interest.chapter_arc: Ch1 dockside passing → Ch2 occasional help, no words → Ch3 guide through flower fields → Ch4 absent but present (her words linger) → Ch5 fishing lesson + journal reading (core chapter) → Ch6 lighthouse lighting moment
romantic_interest.signature_object: 渔夫戒 (fisherman's ring) — grandfather's, worn on a cord close to her heart; her grip on it is the emotional tell

npcs.role: Fisherman — the last old resident who never left
personality_keywords: eyes curve like a crescent moon when smiling
npcs.relation_to_ritsu: Like a grandfather who never gives advice but is always present; first person Ritsu meets on the island

personality_keywords: speech like a hammer — precise and weighty
npcs.relation_to_ritsu: Her question 'Was there ever one ad you truly wanted to write?' leaves Ritsu silent for a long time

personality_keywords: talks a lot but not annoying
npcs.relation_to_ritsu: First person Ritsu has a real conversation with on the island

npcs.role: Lighthouse keeper's widow — true sender of the letter
personality_keywords: has waited long but without urgency
npcs.relation_to_ritsu: Emotional anchor of Ritsu's entire journey; her final words: 'The island waited for you — and you've been waiting for yourself for a long time too'

chapters.color_mood: pale blue + cream, low saturation, bright
chapters.signature_moment: First deep breath in the sea wind; Hinata and Ritsu cross paths on the dock, each carrying luggage

chapters.color_mood: warm yellow + orange-brown, afternoon stone-paved street
chapters.hinata_presence: occasional — passes by, helps briefly, then leaves
chapters.signature_moment: Fountain flows again; fallen leaf spirals once around the jet and drifts away

chapters.hinata_presence: guide — walks every path with Ritsu, tells stories of grandfather's planted trees
chapters.signature_moment: Flower fields bloom; Naho hands Ritsu a half-open bouquet without a word, only a smile

chapters.color_mood: deep green + brown, dappled shade, evening cool
chapters.hinata_presence: absent — but her words 'The island won't rush you' echo
chapters.signature_moment: Campfire first lit; Ritsu looks up at the starry sky, places his hat on his knee

chapters.color_mood: pearl white + mist blue, mineral-scented air
chapters.hinata_presence: core — teaches fishing; Ritsu reads Wataru's journal aloud; fisherman's ring gripped in her palm
chapters.signature_moment: First catch; Hinata glances sideways with a slight smile: 'Not bad.' — Ritsu remembers those two words for a long time

chapters.color_mood: deep night blue + warm gold light, gold column on the sea
chapters.hinata_presence: climax — stands at lighthouse stairs, eyes closed, fisherman's ring in her palm, slow exhale
chapters.signature_moment: Lighthouse beam sweeps the night sea for the first time, lighting all restored areas; Fuyuko whispers Wataru's name; Hinata and Ritsu stand in the same light, nothing said, everything said

memory_fragment_system.description: Six lighthouse diary entries by Maruyama Wataru, unlocked one per chapter completion
memory_fragment_system.format: Handwritten log-style, written in different years; each resonates with Ritsu's current repair experience
memory_fragment_system.hinata_connection: Entries occasionally show young Hinata visiting — her summer arrivals, an afternoon she fell asleep on the lighthouse steps
memory_fragment_system.purpose: Constructs Wataru's complete life of vigil; explains why he wrote the letter: he believed the island was worth waiting for

environment_narrative_principles: Color leads — saturation rises with each repair stage, visual awakening before dialogue
Small details carry big emotion — a blue yukata on the line, a stray cat at the dock, Umeko's kiln light on again
NPC body language over dialogue — Kenzo casts his repaired rod in silence; Fuyuko slowly removes her key ring and places it at the lighthouse base
Sound arrives before visuals — distant wind chimes in morning breeze signal restoration before the scene updates
Lighthouse as emotional anchor — every repair adds a little more light to the lighthouse window; the question 'When will it light up?' drives the whole arc
